description: Most complicated project and how you handled uncertainty
response: One of my most complex projects was architecting an Android diagnostic library for vehicle communication protocols while keeping it reusable across multiple applications. The challenge was balancing hardware-level constraints, clean architecture boundaries, and testability. I decomposed the implementation into domain-driven modules, introduced contract tests early, and iterated with backend and QA stakeholders to validate assumptions quickly.

description: What are you the most proud of (feature/app)?
response: One of the features I'm proud the most is an Android Library for decoding UDP message packages that were coming from a P-Can device (industrial realm), into an Android App listening to these packages stream, throughout a Flask web service installed on my local computer. It was technically complicated, but, also, was the first impacting project that I built supported by AI, in this case LLM Claude 3.7 (this was on summer 2025). I had a lot of fun and learnt a lot back then! :)
